questions.question_text: All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy, ......?
You cannot swim in the sea, ...... you?
It is no use ...... over spilt milk.
Some of the pineapples are ...... sour to be eaten.
......, learners must behave well.

options.B: The last but not the least

questions.question_text: Where ...... Asana and Betty spending their next holidays?
The messenger did not ...... anyone in the house.
She lives in her ...... house.
Their sister wore a ...... to the party.
It was ...... late to go out alone.
questions.explanation:
Too goes with to 

So goes with that

questions.question_text: This is the boy ...... rescued us.
questions.explanation:
Who vs Whom

If in doubt, reword the clause to see which personal pronoun you need: he/him, she/her, etc. Then replace that pronoun with who or whom, using the following rule:

Use WHO for he, she, they, we:

The people who just boarded the plane are in a rock band.

(Reword: They just boarded th

questions.question_text: The nurse made me ...... a lot of water.
questions.explanation:
Causative Verb (make)

Subject + make (any tense) + object (always person) + base form of verb .

She made me take up this course. I was never up for this.

The police made them surrender and reveal their plans.

questions.question_text: It is not easy to identify ...... voice it was.
questions.explanation:
Who's vs Whose

who + is or who + has = who’s.

Example: Who’s hungry?

 Whose is the possessive form of the pronoun who. Use it when you’re asking (or declaring) to whom something belongs.

questions.question_text: Neither the parents nor the child ..... at home yesterday.
questions.explanation:
Neither nor and Either or

When we use either…or and neither…nor, we present a choice between two different options. The suggestion is that one of these options will complete the action, so when we have a choice between two singular objects, the verb can only be done by one of them. Hence, though we

questions.question_text: Her clothes would look better if she ...... them.
questions.explanation:
The sentence is a conditional sentence that talks about an unreal situation in the present. In such cases, we use the past simple tense in the if-clause and 'would' + base verb in the main clause.

questions.question_text: We will not allow him to dampen our spirits.
The commotion at the stadium was avoidable.
What transpired between them remains a secret.
Lance only talks about trivial matters.
The robbers ransacked the house.
I was informed at the eleventh hour about his decision to leave town. This means that I heard it
questions.explanation:
The eleventh hour

The last moment or almost too late

questions.question_text: We tried all we could to amuse her but she kept a straight face . This means that she
questions.explanation:
A straight face

A blank or serious facial expression, especially when trying not to laugh.

 Example : My father kept a straight face when he joked.

questions.question_text: When we went to Nina's house, we were told that she had gone away bag and baggage . This means that Nina had gone away
questions.explanation:
Bag and baggage

All of one's belongings, especially with reference to departing with them; completely, totally. For example, The day he quit his job, John walked out, bag and baggage.

questions.question_text: Samantha's main problem is that she can see no further than her nose . This means that Samantha
questions.explanation:
See no further than the end of one's nose

a. to be short-sighted; have myopia

b. to lack insight or foresight

questions.question_text: When he lost his job, Yaro was left to sink or swim . This means that Yaro

options.C: had to survive on his own.

questions.explanation:
Sink or swim

If you are left to sink or swim, you are given no help so that you succeed or fail completely by your own efforts: My employer gave me no help when I started my new job - I was just left to sink or swim.

questions.question_text: She accidentally tore her dress.
The story was written in simple language.
The arrogant storekeeper lost all his customers.
We finished several projects last year.
The unfavourable weather affected their health.
